meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Learn how to use idea validation to overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to master your first project.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting syntax errors by validating your core logic before coding. By using Antigravity to define business rules and Claude Code to scaffold the implementation, you shift from 'debugging code' to 'orchestrating solutions.' This architectural shift ensures your app works on the first try, saving hours of frustration.
content.intro:
The screen is a blur of red text. You’ve spent six hours chasing a logic error that feels like it’s mocking you, and your motivation is evaporating faster than your coffee. Every student hits this wall, but the mistake isn't in your syntax—it's in your approach to building.

True engineering isn't about fixing broken code; it's about building code that cannot break. By shifting your focus to idea validation and architectural integrity, you stop being a frantic bug-hunter and start being a confident builder. Let’s transform your workflow and turn those errors into milestones.

steps.title: Define the Core Logic
steps.description: Before writing a single line of implementation, define the inputs and expected outputs of your primary feature to ensure the logic holds water.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature requirement: [Insert Feature]. Create a structured JSON schema for the data flow and identify three potential edge cases that would cause a runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'create a file named schema.json based on the logic defined in the Antigravity validation output'

steps.title: Architect the Scaffold
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the directory structure and boilerplate, ensuring your project follows industry-standard patterns from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a folder structure for a [Your App Type] that prioritizes modularity and error handling. List the purpose of each directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'init project with directory structure: [Paste Antigravity Structure]'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Logic
steps.description: Write your functions with built-in validation checks to catch errors at the source rather than at the runtime level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to include guard clauses and type checking for all inputs: [Your Code Snippet]
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'apply the refactored logic to index.ts and run tests'

steps.title: Automated Error Simulation
steps.description: Use your tools to simulate failure states, ensuring your application fails gracefully rather than crashing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that simulates these three failure scenarios: [List Scenarios].
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'run the generated test suite and log all failed assertions'

steps.title: Final Integration Review
steps.description: Perform a final audit of the interaction between your validated modules to ensure no unexpected state leakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the integrated code for state leakage and consistency. Suggest improvements for memory efficiency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'perform code review on main branch and provide summary of potential bottlenecks'

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as a roadmap, not a failure; they are the most descriptive documentation you have.
Always validate your data schema before writing the database integration layer.
Use 'dry-run' modes in your CLI tools to verify logic before committing to production.
If you find yourself debugging for more than 30 minutes, stop, step away, and re-validate your initial assumptions.
Focus on building 'pure' functions that have no side effects; they are significantly easier to test and debug.
resources: MDN Web Docs for Logic Debugging: https://developer.mozilla.org
content.next_steps: Once your first project is stable, move to implementing unit tests using Vitest to automate your validation process permanently.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. The only thing left is to commit the code and ship your vision. Stop debugging in the dark and start building with intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
